ALTHOUGH SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ARE DESIRABLE, THEY ARE NOT MANDATORY TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. DECEDENT'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND THE OTHER EVIDENCE PRESENTED IS SUFFICIENT TO DO SO. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE GIVING RISE TO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS FOR THE COURT TO CONSIDER A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION AND FOR A CONTINUANCE WERE DENIED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ABBOTT STREET RADIATOR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PERSUASION. DEFENDANT'S DECLARATIONS FAIL TO SET FORTH THE REQUISITE FOUNDATIONAL FACTS UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTIONS 1272 AND 403 TO SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY THAT DECEDENT DID NOT WORK OR DO BUSINESS WITH ABBOTT OR EXPLAIN HOW IT IS THE DECLARANTS KNOW THAT ABBOTT DID NOT MAKE OR USE ASBESTOS PRODUCTS PRIOR TO THEIR WORKING FOR THE COMPANY OR HOW IT IS THEY LEARNED WHAT PRODUCTS THE COMPANY MADE AND THE COMPONENTS THEREOF AFTER THEY JOINED THE COMPANY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Bruce Jackson, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP 818/342-7442 for pltfs & opposing parties; Michelle M. Clowser, Esq. of Brydon Hugo & Parker 808-0300 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant's reliance on plaintiff's objections to special interrogatories does not shift the burden of producing evidence and the Kentfield Declaration lacks the foundational facts to do so. Court will rule on evidentiary objections. Plaintiff to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. DEFENDANT ABBOTT STREET RADIATOR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Duplicate entry. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrea Avila of Patane Gumberg (831.755.1461) for defendant and moving party
Bruce Jackson of Keller Fishback & Jackson (818.342.7442) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued. Defendant's requests for the Court to consider a supplemental declaration and for a continuance were denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ABBOTT STREET RADIATOR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied for failure to shift the burden of persuasion. Defendant's declarations fail to set forth the requisite foundational facts under Evidence Code sections 1272 and 403 to support their testimony that decedent did not work or do business with Abbott or explain how it is the declarants know that Abbott did not make or use asbestos products prior to their working for the company or how it is they learned what products the company made and the components thereof after they joined the company. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bruce Jackson of Keller Fishback & Jackson (818.342.7442) for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative as follows: DEFENDANT J.T. THORPE & SONS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of producing evidence for defendant may not simply rely on discovery responses where plaintiff has filed objections or has not provided a verification and the testimony decedent's widow in inadequate. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jack Henning of Dillingham & Murphy (397.2700) for defendant and moving party
Bruce Jackson of Keller Fishback & Jackson (818.342.7442) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DEERE & COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof under McGonnel v. Kaiser Gypsum Co. (2002) 98 Cal. App. 4th 1098. Although special interrogatories are desirable, they are not mandatory to shift the burden of producing evidence. Decedent's deposition testimony and the other evidence presented is sufficient to do so. Plaintiff fails to present evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
